_meta.title: Pulse Of Raga
_meta.subtitle: A Carnatic raga explorer · scales, master recordings, and 5-axis fusion matching

ragas.name: Mohanam
ragas.tagline: The major pentatonic — sunlit, open, instantly singable.
ragas.mood: bright, joyful, auspicious; uplifting and calming
ragas.time_of_day: no strong traditional association (often morning per modern convention)

fusion.prompt: nu-disco fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Mohanam. The lead melody strictly follows raga Mohanam: ascending S R2 G3 P D2 S, descending S D2 P G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full nu-disco production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of nu-disco. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: bright, joyful, auspicious; uplifting and calming. Around 112 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Raga Mohanam is pentatonic — it maps naturally onto Western pop/EDM shapes.

deep house fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Mohanam. The lead melody strictly follows raga Mohanam: ascending S R2 G3 P D2 S, descending S D2 P G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full deep house production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of deep house. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: bright, joyful, auspicious; uplifting and calming. Around 122 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Raga Mohanam is pentatonic — it maps naturally onto Western pop/EDM shapes.

ragas.name: Hamsadhwani
ragas.tagline: The swan-song raga — every Carnatic concert opens here.
ragas.mood: joyous, auspicious, energetic — 'the sound of swans'

Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full uplifting house production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of uplifting house. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. ragas.name: Bhairavi
ragas.tagline: Boundless devotion and pathos — the raga of M.S. Subbulakshmi.
ragas.mood: deep devotion and tranquility with cosmic earthiness
ragas.time_of_day: no strong traditional association (different from Hindustani Bhairavi)

fusion.why: earthy, textural, percussive — a natural Indian-fusion lane
fusion.prompt: organic house fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Bhairavi. The lead melody strictly follows raga Bhairavi: ascending S R2 G2 M1 P D2 N2 S, descending S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full organic house production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of organic house. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: deep devotion and tranquility with cosmic earthiness. Around 120 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.

progressive electronic fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Bhairavi. Underneath, full progressive electronic production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of progressive electronic. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.

ragas.mood: majestic, auspicious, graceful; 'mother who dispels fear'

ragas.name: Hanumatodi
ragas.tagline: Deep, complex pathos — the connoisseur's raga.
ragas.mood: deep pathos with profound devotion; heavy and emotionally complex
ragas.time_of_day: no strong traditional association (often rendered as a main raga in concerts)

fusion.prompt: trip-hop fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Hanumatodi. The lead melody strictly follows raga Hanumatodi: ascending S R1 G2 M1 P D1 N2 S, descending S N2 D1 P M1 G2 R1 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Underneath, full trip-hop production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of trip-hop. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. ragas.name: Kambodhi
ragas.tagline: Regal and oceanic — grandeur with feminine grace.
ragas.mood: grand, majestic, regal devotion with feminine grace
ragas.time_of_day: evening / first quarter of night (older treatise per Sivatattvaratnakara)

The lead melody strictly follows raga Kambodhi: ascending S R2 G3 M1 P D2 S, descending S N2 D2 P M1 G3 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Underneath, full romantic R&B production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of romantic R&B. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. ragas.name: Kanada
ragas.tagline: Grave and tender — compassion welling up from love.
ragas.mood: grave, emotional, weighty; compassion from overflowing love

fusion.prompt: emotional piano-electronic fused with South Indian Carnatic classical melody in raga Kanada. The lead melody strictly follows raga Kanada: ascending S R2 G2 M1 D2 N2 S, descending S N2 P M1 G2 M1 R2 S, with ornamented, gliding gamakam phrasing (meend-style slides between notes). Carry the raga line on continuous-pitch voices — solo voice, Carnatic violin, veena, or bamboo flute — never piano. Underneath, full emotional piano-electronic production: modern drums, bass and texture characteristic of emotional piano-electronic. Tanpura drone and mridangam accents bridge the two worlds. Mood: grave, emotional, weighty; compassion from overflowing love. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Honour the raga's vakra (zig-zag) phrasing — the note order is deliberately crooked, not a straight scale. It is a bhashanga raga — a foreign note appears as brief colour; keep it a passing touch, never a resting note.

Mood: grave, emotional, weighty; compassion from overflowing love. Around 88 BPM. Keep the raga line monophonic and melodic; do not force Western chord changes onto it. Honour the raga's vakra (zig-zag) phrasing — the note order is deliberately crooked, not a straight scale.
